title: adiantum amplum var. brasiliense
intro: Adiantum amplum var. brasiliense is a variety of the Adiantum amplum plant species, commonly known as the large maidenhair fern. It belongs to the family Adiantaceae and is native to Brazil. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Adiantum amplum var. brasiliense include reducing inflammation, soothing wounds, and promoting overall well-being, with herbal preparations often involving infusions or decoctions of the plant's leaves.
